CREATE TABLE test_list (id INTEGER, values LIST<Integer>);
SELECT id, list_reverse(values) AS reversed_values FROM test_list;
SELECT id, values, list_reverse(values) AS reversed_values FROM test_list WHERE values IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE additional_data (id INTEGER, value INTEGER);
SELECT t.id, list_reverse(t.values) AS reversed_values, ad.value FROM test_list t JOIN additional_data ad ON t.id = ad.id;
SELECT id, list_reverse(list_concat(values, [1, 2, 3])) AS concat_reversed FROM test_list;
SELECT id, list_reverse(list_reverse(values)) AS double_reverse FROM test_list;
SELECT id, list_reverse(values || [100]) AS appended_reversed FROM test_list;
SELECT id, list_reverse(COALESCE(values, [0])) AS reversed_coalesce FROM test_list;
